mappings.reason: Establishing and maintaining an enterprise asset inventory directly satisfies the CCF control's requirement to maintain an information systems inventory and review it on a periodic basis.
Addressing unauthorized assets aligns with the CCF control's reconciliation objective by ensuring that discrepancies (non-inventoried devices) discovered during periodic review are resolved.

Utilizing an active discovery tool provides a specific mechanism class (active network discovery) to identify assets, directly supporting the CCF control's requirement to reconcile the inventory periodically.
Using DHCP logging to update the inventory provides a log-based discovery mechanism that supports the CCF control's requirement to reconcile and maintain an accurate information systems inventory.

Utilizing a passive discovery tool provides an equivalent network discovery mechanism (passive class) to identify assets, directly supporting the CCF control's requirement to reconcile the inventory periodically.
